forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,32 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KTCAFH74MPPZ9282P699QBW0
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8580'
+project: card-comments
+title: Verify comment-add emits a field-level event and lands in the entity store (production-path test)
+---
+## What
+Comments mutate the task's `comments` field via `ectx.write(&task)`, so the entity cache already emits `EntityEvent::EntityChanged` carrying a `FieldChange { field: "comments", value: <new array> }` on its broadcast channel (see `crates/swissarmyhammer-entity/src/events.rs` and the `write`/`write_internal` path in `context.rs` that diffs fields and broadcasts). No NEW event type is needed — this task PROVES the thin field-level event fires for comment add/edit/delete and reaches a subscriber, closing the command → event → store loop the project requires for every new store-affecting feature.
+
+This task is a real-path integration test, not new production code (add production code only if the test reveals the event does NOT fire — e.g. a no-op-write suppression or enrichment issue specific to the comment-log field).
+
+Files:
+- Add an integration test (kanban crate `tests/` or the entity crate, wherever existing EntityChanged-broadcast tests live — find the test referenced near `context.rs` line ~3927 "Exactly one EntityChanged event"). Subscribe to the entity cache broadcast channel, run `AddComment` through the real op path, and assert exactly one `EntityChanged` event arrives whose `changes` contains a `FieldChange` for `field == "comments"` with the new member present in `value`.
+- Add an analogous assertion for `UpdateComment` and `DeleteComment` (the `comments` field change is broadcast each time).
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] An integration test subscribes to the real broadcast channel and observes a `comments` `FieldChange` after `AddComment`.
+- [ ] Edit and delete each produce a `comments` field-level change event.
+- [ ] The event is thin: it carries `{field, value}` only — no enrichment round-trip / extra reads are introduced.
+- [ ] If any production change was required to make the event fire, it is minimal and documented in the test's comments.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `comment_add_emits_field_change_event` — exactly one EntityChanged with a `comments` FieldChange after add.
+- [ ] `comment_edit_and_delete_emit_field_change_events`.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` (and `-p swissarmyhammer-entity` if the test lives there) — green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the subscribe-and-assert test first; it should pass once comment ops exist (dependency), confirming the loop. If it fails, fix the production path minimally.
\ No newline at end of file

 ## What
-Comments mutate the task's `comments` field via `ectx.write(&task)`, so the entity cache already emits `EntityEvent::EntityChanged` carrying a `FieldChange { field: "comments", value: <new array> }` on its broadcast channel (see `crates/swissarmyhammer-entity/src/events.rs` and the `write`/`write_internal` path in `context.rs` that diffs fields and broadcasts). +Comments mutate the task's `comments` field via `ectx.write(&task)`, so the entity cache already emits `EntityEvent::EntityChanged` carrying a `FieldChange { field: "comments", value: <new array> }` on its broadcast channel. CONFIRMED shape: `crates/swissarmyhammer-entity/src/events.rs` defines `EntityEvent::EntityChanged { changes: Vec<FieldChange> }` (events.rs:38-48), FieldChange is the thin field-level shape (events.rs:21), removals encoded as `FieldChange { value: Null }`. No NEW event type is needed — this task PROVES the thin field-level event fires for comment changes via BOTH write paths and reaches a subscriber, closing the command → event → store loop the project requires for every new store-affecting feature.

-This task is a real-path integration test, not new production code (add production code only if the test reveals the event does NOT fire — e.g. a no-op-write suppression or enrichment issue specific to the comment-log field).
+There are two write paths and the test must cover both:
+- **Agent op path**: `AddComment` / `UpdateComment` / `DeleteComment` (depends on the agent-dispatch task).
+- **UI field-set path**: `UpdateEntityField` on the `comments` field (depends on the normalization task) — this is the path the React editor actually exercises.

+This task is a real-path integration test, not new production code (add production code only if the test reveals the event does NOT fire — e.g. a no-op-write suppression that swallows the comments change).
+
 Files:
-- Add an integration test (kanban crate `tests/` or the entity crate, wherever existing EntityChanged-broadcast tests live — find the test referenced near `context.rs` line ~3927 "Exactly one EntityChanged event"). Subscribe to the entity cache broadcast channel, run `AddComment` through the real op path, and assert exactly one `EntityChanged` event arrives whose `changes` contains a `FieldChange` for `field == "comments"` with the new member present in `value`.
-- Add an analogous assertion for `UpdateComment` and `DeleteComment` (the `comments` field change is broadcast each time).
+- Add integration tests where existing EntityChanged-broadcast tests live (find the test near `context.rs` ~line 3927 "Exactly one EntityChanged event"). Subscribe to the entity cache broadcast channel, run the comment mutation through the real path, assert exactly one `EntityChanged` whose `changes` contains a `FieldChange` for `field == "comments"` with the expected member present in `value`.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] An integration test subscribes to the real broadcast channel and observes a `comments` `FieldChange` after `AddComment`.
-- [ ] Edit and delete each produce a `comments` field-level change event.
-- [ ] The event is thin: it carries `{field, value}` only — no enrichment round-trip / extra reads are introduced.
-- [ ] If any production change was required to make the event fire, it is minimal and documented in the test's comments.
+- [ ] Agent path: `AddComment` produces exactly one `EntityChanged` with a `comments` `FieldChange`; edit and delete each produce one too.
+- [ ] UI path: `UpdateEntityField` committing a new `comments` array produces a `comments` `FieldChange` carrying the normalized array.
+- [ ] The event is thin: `{field, value}` only — no enrichment round-trip / extra reads introduced.
+- [ ] Any production change required to make the event fire is minimal and documented in the test comments.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `comment_add_emits_field_change_event` — exactly one EntityChanged with a `comments` FieldChange after add.
-- [ ] `comment_edit_and_delete_emit_field_change_events`.
-- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` (and `-p swissarmyhammer-entity` if the test lives there) — green.
+- [ ] `comment_add_emits_field_change_event` (agent op path).
+- [ ] `comment_edit_and_delete_emit_field_change_events` (agent op path).
+- [ ] `comment_field_set_emits_field_change_event` (UI `UpdateEntityField` path).
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` (and `-p swissarmyhammer-entity` if a test lives there) — green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write the subscribe-and-assert test first; it should pass once comment ops exist (dependency), confirming the loop. If it fails, fix the production path minimally.
\ No newline at end of file
